title: [cross-impl] IID derivation diverges between Rust and Python/C
description: Rust (lichen-link/src/identity.rs:24) uses keyed hash_32 twice per project-LICHEN-swvz update. Python (crypto/identity.py:138: sha256(pubkey).digest()[:8]) and C (lichen/subsys/lichen/l2/ipv6_addr.c:90: lichen_sha256 + memcpy first 8 bytes) both follow SHA-256(pubkey)[:8] + clear U/L bit. spec/03-addressing.md:15-16 specifies the SHA-256 method as source of truth. Breaks human_address, IPv6 IID consistency, and interop. Test vectors for addresses will fail across langs.
